name: Function Call With Incorrect Order of Arguments
description: The product calls a function, procedure, or routine with the arguments specified in an incorrect order, leading to resultant weaknesses. This commonly occurs through programmer mistakes like typos or copy-paste errors, especially in languages lacking strong type enforcement.
detection_pattern: TRANSFORM[function_call] node passes arguments to EXTERNAL or TRANSFORM[callee] in an order that does not match the expected parameter signature defined in META[function_signature], potentially swapping security-critical values
